channel.title: Comments for page "How Is Your Name?"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "How Is Your Name?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
CW: Shameless fangirling
Not that anyone would expect otherwise from me at this point

When I first met the Fellow Scholar I had wild speculations in re: what manner of grand caper would be necessary to liberate his name. This surpassed my imagination by an order of magnitude, and I'm only sorry I was inactive and did not get a chance to read it hot off the presses.

Isabel V, Manny and Iris are all characters I love intensely for completely contradictory reasons. Seeing Iris at any kind of disadvantage, even temporarily, had me simultaneously distraught at the thought of any harm coming to her and deeply satisfied to see a proud and ruthless person have that same ruthlessness inflicted on them by someone else. Manny once again came through as a man as ethical as he is tough. The additional bits of Manny lore sprinkled in there (irrational as this may sound) felt as though they were a delicious present just for me. And Isabel had me cackling in vicarious glee on numerous occasions.

As is often the case with your stuff, there were many many lines that I wanted to cut out and study and go "how can I write me some goddamn zingers like that, hot damn." Particularly, thought not exclusively, those relating to: Nominative magic. Thaumo-nuclear heelys. Communication solely through malevolent machine noises and/or weaponized subconscious poetry recitation.

A perfectly balanced multi-GOI romp complete with nailbiting tension, zany twists, satisfying explosions and an appropriately Wonderous balloon.

*chef kiss*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Top hats being invented by IngSoc! Corgis not existing! And of course getting a certain rabbit's Name back … I like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, this was just a delightful read. So many things I enjoy wrapped up in a delightful little package. Would (and will) read again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Something I've wondered: how does Isabel pronounce her own name? Specifically, is she "Wondertainment the Fifth" or "Wondertainment Vee"? Because honestly, both seem in character.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When I put them all together like this its really obvious how many characters I've stolen over the years.

If you didn't keep making awesome adaptations of these characters, we wouldn't keep upvoting them :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Initially, Icky will be furious with him for risking his life without even telling her. The Circus needs him for his various mind powers, plus his death would also be terrible for morale since she and Lolly and pretty much everyone else loves him.

But she also knows him very well, so when she - eventually - calms down, she'll understand why he felt this was something he had to do. She still might be peeved that he didn't tell her, but he didn't tell her because he cares about her and wouldn't want her risking her life for something that he felt was solely his responsibility. The only reason he asked Isabel-V is because he knew how much she hated The Factory and how eager she'd be to shoot it up.

Icky will eventually understand this and forgive him, but she might make him earn it a bit first though. She will, at the least, be happy with the end results against The Factory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
“Elevators are always nice to me,” Dr. Isabel Helga Anastasia Parvati Wondertainment V explained. “I think it’s because I always end up breaking escalators, and they regard escalators as their natural enemies.”

This was hilarious. And the whole tale was also really enjoyable overall!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this so much!

Though I don't think Icky will be too angry, will she? Maybe upset that Manny did something so dangerous without telling her. But I think she'll approve of this incarnation of Isabella Wondertainment. She seems almost Clown-like. And I can't imagine Icky would disapprove of anyone inspiring Factory workers to revolt against their sadistic masters, given that Icky and Manny led the revolt against Fuller.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've read this at least 3 times now and I keep finding more to love. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
‘Wolves asleep amidst the trees
Bats all a swaying in the breeze
But one soul lies anxious wide awake
Fearing all manner of ghouls, hags and wraiths.’

Now that’s not a verse I ever expected to find on this site.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Wolves asleep amidst the trees.
Bats all a swaying in the breeze.
But one soul lies anxious wide awake.
Fearing all manner of ghouls, hags and wraiths.

So, this tale's a little longer than my usual stuff. That wasn't really planned, it just came out like that. What is more typical is that it's dialogue-heavy, lore-heavy, and highly dependent on pre-existing works on the site. I understand that this will limit its appeal to new readers, but I'm okay with that as I felt that this was a tale I had to write.

▶ Click for collapsed long post:  
▼

Isabel-V was always a good fit for Dread & Circuses, but I deliberately avoided using her since, as mentioned in the tale itself, my cross-overs with DarkStuff's Wonder World canon made it kind of confusing. But then Darkstuff wrote SCP-445-EX, and I honestly didn't like the idea of a Dr. Wondertainment who could have their Wonderlight snuffed out by modern world politics.

Unlike Darkstuff, I like the concept of the Wondermaker, of a primal cosmic entity on par with Mekhane and Yalbadaoth who continuously re-incarnates like the Avatar. I like the concept of the Wondermaker being the opposite of the Scarlet King. The Scarlet King saw that the World was horrible, and took that as licence to be horrible himself. The Wondermaker saw the World was horrible, and decided to make something wondrous to make it a little less so, and maybe if they kept at it for long enough the World wouldn't be so horrible any more. And so, Isabel-V (And Reginald-III before her) is now officially the Dr. Wondertainment of Dread & Circuses, and she is the Wondermaker, and she did stab the Scarlet King in the face. She also has a horrifyingly childish sense of black and white morality, but that's neither here nor there.

Of course, that does make the situation with Wonder World kind of unclear, even more so with Darkstuff writing his own Isabel-V tale after reading this as a draft, and then posting before me even after promising me he wouldn't. Bottom line, this tale isn't Wonder World Canon, and The Really Really Real Adventures in Capitalism isn't Dread & Circuses, and the Isabel-V in this tale is not Mrs. Redd. Within Dread & Circuses, Wonder WorldTM was an independent subsidiary of the Workshops of Wonder that ended up breaking away entirely and denying that Reggie or Isabel were the Wondermaker because the real Wondermaker is the friends we made along the way, and assigned the honorary of Dr. Wondertainment to their CEO.

Special thanks goes to PeppersGhost for creating SCP-4000 and The Man With The Upside-Down Face, To Metaphysician for creating the Investor and for Stormbreath's depiction of him, to djoric for creating Isabel-V (or at least Adventures in Capitalism) and to Randomini for creating Iris Dark (Did he create Isabel-V or just Mr. Redd? I'm can't bother to check right now). When I put them all together like this its really obvious how many characters I've stolen over the years.

Additional thanks to all the beta readers on this, including Darkstuff. Treacherous, treacherous Darkstuff.
